title: penstemon ophianthus
intro: Penstemon ophianthus, also known as Ophian's beardtongue, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the plantain family (Plantaginaceae) and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as wound healing, fever reduction, and respiratory problems, due to its expectorant and bronchodilatory properties. Penstemon ophianthus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory infections.
